title: Support shortened version of $timeformat(-9, 0, "")
body:
Follow up to #2253

What would you like added/supported?

shortened version of `$timeformat(-9, 0, "")`

```
diff --git a/test_regress/t/t_display_time.v b/test_regress/t/t_display_time.v
index 72b84a94c..d422e28fb 100644
--- a/test_regress/t/t_display_time.v
+++ b/test_regress/t/t_display_time.v
@@ -18,7 +18,7 @@ module t (/*AUTOARG*/
          $write;  // Check missing arguments work
          $write("default:   [%0t] 0t time [%t] No0 time  p=%p 0p=%0p\n",
                 $time, $time, $time, $time);
-         $timeformat(-9, 0, "",   0);
+         $timeformat(-9, 0, "");
          $write("-9,0,,0:   [%0t] 0t time [%t] No0 time  p=%p 0p=%0p\n",
                 $time, $time, $time, $time);
          $timeformat(-9, 0, "",   10);
```

```
%Error: t/t_display_time.v:21:31: syntax error, unexpected ')'
   21 |          $timeformat(-9, 0, "");
      |                               ^
```

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?

`v5.037-g49fe12963`

Can you attach an example that runs on other simulators?  (Must be openly licensed, completely self-contained and self-checking so can directly run what you provide. Ideally use test_regress format, see https://veripool.org/guide/latest/contributing.html?highlight=test_regress#reporting-bugs

Works with VCS.
